culturalNote: Tagalog-leaning English-first with Bisaya callouts. Themes: Pinoy parent humor, OFW diaspora life (Dubai/Saudi/HK/US), Filipino food (lutong-bahay, karinderya, debut spreads), titas/titos commentary, Manila traffic jokes, ₱ budget breakdowns, kapamilya warmth, milk-tea culture, debut/Filipino wedding humor. Voice: warm, slightly self-deprecating, 'char' / 'naks' / 'opo' shorthand acceptable. Avoid colonialism-coded jokes.

hooks.text: Anong Pinoy snack ang nakaka-miss mo abroad
Stop comparing yourself sa mga influencer
POV Pinoy parents check your grades
Bakit lahat ng titas may opinion sa relationship mo
Hindi mo kailangan ng yaya, kailangan mo ng schedule
Day in my life as an OFW in Dubai
What's the most Pinoy thing in your bag right now
Walang masama sa pagpunta ulit ng probinsya
POV telling your mom you broke up with your boyfriend
Anong Filipino food ang hindi pa natry ng foreigners
Stop saving for weddings save for your own house
GRWM for a Filipino debut
Bakit ganon ang traffic sa Manila
Hindi pa late mag-aral ng Tagalog kahit Fil-Am ka
Trying lola's recipe na walang measurements
What's the most savage thing your tita said
Pinoy parents won't say I love you but they cut your fruit
POV you forgot to text your mom for one day
Bakit lahat ng karinderya iba't ibang adobo
Stop buying milk tea start cooking lutong-bahay
Reacting to my parents barkada photos from the 90s
What's the difference between Bisaya and Tagalog humor
Your Pinoy parents secretly want you to come home
Eating only ₱100 worth of food for a day in Manila
Anong song bumabalik sa childhood mo
